steps.title: Look for tight clustering
steps.content: The smallest standard deviation comes from the data set whose values are closest to their mean. The values $46,47,47,47,48$ have three values at the center and the others only $1$ away.

steps.title: Compare the spreads
steps.content: The ranges are $6$, $4$, $4$, and $2$ for choices A through D. The smallest range and tightest clustering are in choice D. The answer is D.
